risk_notes: Flask dependency is imported but not pinned to any version; no requirements.txt or pyproject.toml shown.
No lockfile or version manifest provided in the agent output, so transitive dependencies are entirely unresolved.
Hardcoded seed credentials/tokens ('admin-token-123', 'user-token-456') inserted into the database via app.py — secrets embedded in source.
Flask app launched with debug=True in app.py __main__ block, which enables the Werkzeug debugger (RCE risk if exposed).
sqlite3 is used from the Python standard library (not a third-party dep) — no supply-chain risk, noted for completeness.
init_db() runs at import time of app.py, which can cause unintended DB writes during tooling/import scans.

supply_chain_meaning: Static manifest/install-pattern scan; LLM dependency extraction from agent output; OSV lookup attempted (no matching advisories or no pinned versions).
